meta.title: The Teachings of Jesus Christ
meta.subtitle: A Comprehensive Categorization of His Words as Recorded in the New Testament
scope: The four Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke, John) — including parables, discourses, and dialogue
The institution of the Lord's Supper as quoted by Paul (1 Corinthians 11:23–25)
The letters to the seven churches and other direct speech in Revelation (Revelation 1–3, 22)
Damascus road appearance of Jesus to Saul (Acts 9:4–6; 22:7–10; 26:14–18)
Vision to Ananias commissioning Paul's mission (Acts 9:10–16)
Vision to Paul in the Temple (Acts 22:18–21)
Vision to Paul in Jerusalem custody (Acts 23:11)
Jesus speaks to Paul: 'My grace is sufficient for you' (2 Cor 12:9)
Heavenly voice to Peter on clean and unclean (Acts 10:13–15; 11:7–9)
Commission to write to the seven churches (Rev 1:11)
Jesus explains the vision of stars and lampstands (Rev 1:19–20)
Eschatological warning: 'I am coming like a thief' (Rev 16:15)
'Behold, I am making all things new' — new creation declaration (Rev 21:5–8)
Alpha and Omega self-declaration: 'I am the Alpha and the Omega... who is to come, the Almighty' (Rev 1:8)

teachings.text: If earthly fathers give good gifts, how much more will the heavenly Father give good things to those who ask
teachings.quote: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?

teachings.text: Not one sparrow falls without the Father's knowledge — even the hairs of your head are numbered; you are worth more
teachings.quote: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father. But the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.

teachings.text: Your Father knows what you need before you ask; he sees what is done in secret and rewards it
teachings.quote: Be not ye therefore like unto them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of, before ye ask him.

teachings.text: Call no man your father on earth — you have one Father, who is in heaven
teachings.quote: And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.

teachings.text: God is Spirit — those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth; the Father seeks such worshipers
teachings.quote: But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him. God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.

teachings.text: Tells the Samaritan woman true worship will not be tied to any place — salvation comes from the Jews
teachings.quote: Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father. Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.

teachings.text: God so loved the world he gave his Son — not to condemn the world but to save it; whoever believes will not perish
teachings.quote: ¶ For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life. For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.

teachings.text: With God all things are possible; the Father raises the dead and gives life to whom he will
teachings.quote: With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.

teachings.text: No one can come to the Son unless the Father draws him — all the Father gives will come; none can be snatched from his hand
teachings.quote: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out. No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.

teachings.text: The Father himself loves those who love Jesus; he has granted the Son authority over all judgment
teachings.quote: For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.

teachings.text: Thanks the Father for hiding these things from the wise and revealing them to little children
teachings.quote: I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes. Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.

categories.title: The Identity of Jesus Christ
categories.description: Jesus made explicit claims about his own nature, origin, authority, and mission that form the theological core of his teaching.

teachings.text: I AM the bread of life — whoever comes to me shall not hunger; whoever believes shall not thirst
teachings.quote: I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst. I am that bread of life. I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.

teachings.text: I AM the light of the world — whoever follows me will not walk in darkness
teachings.quote: I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.

teachings.text: I AM the door / gate — whoever enters through me will be saved
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep. I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.

teachings.text: I AM the good shepherd — I know my sheep and lay down my life for them
teachings.quote: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep. I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine.

teachings.text: I AM the resurrection and the life — whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live
teachings.quote: I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:

teachings.text: I AM the way, the truth, and the life — no one comes to the Father except through me
teachings.quote: I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.

teachings.text: I AM the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser
teachings.quote: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman. I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.

teachings.text: Before Abraham was, I AM
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.

teachings.text: I AM the Alpha and the Omega, the root and offspring of David, the bright morning star
teachings.quote: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last. I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, and the bright and morning star.

teachings.text: Warns that those who do not believe he is who he claims will die in their sins
teachings.quote: I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins: for if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins. Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning. I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have heard of him.

teachings.text: Contrasts the true shepherd who enters by the door and is known by his sheep with the thief who climbs in another way
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber. But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep. To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out. And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice. And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.

teachings.text: All who came before him are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them
teachings.quote: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.

teachings.text: The hireling, who is not the shepherd, sees the wolf coming and flees because he does not own the sheep, leaving them to be caught and scattered
teachings.quote: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep. The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.

teachings.text: Promises to lay down his life for the sheep and to gather other sheep not of this fold into one flock under one shepherd
teachings.quote: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep. And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd.

teachings.text: I and the Father are one — the Father is in me and I am in the Father
teachings.quote: I and my Father are one. But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.

teachings.text: He who has seen me has seen the Father — I do nothing on my own but speak as the Father taught me
teachings.quote: Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?

teachings.text: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hand — all things have been delivered to me; no one knows the Son except the Father
The Father is greater than I; those who honor the Son honor the Father who sent him
teachings.quote: Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.

teachings.text: As a boy in the temple, asks his parents why they searched — he must be about his Father's business
teachings.quote: How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father’s business?

teachings.text: He defends his Sabbath healing by declaring that his Father works until now and he himself works
teachings.quote: My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.

teachings.text: Teaches that the Son acts only in concert with the Father, who loves the Son and shows him all his works
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise. For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.

teachings.text: Names his witnesses — John the Baptist, his own works, and the Father — to confirm his judgment is just
teachings.quote: I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me. If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true. ¶ There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true. Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth. But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved. He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light. ¶ But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me. And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape. And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.

teachings.text: His teaching is not his own but the Father's — anyone willing to do God's will can recognize it as divine
teachings.quote: My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me. If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself. He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him. Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?

teachings.text: Tells them they judge by the flesh while he judges no one — yet his judgment is true because the Father is with him
teachings.quote: Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man. And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.

teachings.text: He tells them they neither know him nor his Father; if they had known him, they would have known the Father also
teachings.quote: Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.

teachings.text: His honor comes from the Father — they claim God but do not know him, while the Son knows him and keeps his word
teachings.quote: If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God: Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.

teachings.text: He asks for which of the many good works shown them from the Father do they seek to stone him
teachings.quote: Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?

teachings.text: Cites Psalm 82 to defend his claim — if Scripture calls others 'gods,' why call it blasphemy when the Son of God claims it?
teachings.quote: Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods? If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken; Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?

teachings.text: To see and believe in the Son is to see and believe in the Father who sent him — he came as light to save, not to judge
teachings.quote: He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me. And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me. I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness. And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

teachings.text: Affirms his words come from the Father, whose commandment is everlasting life
teachings.quote: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak. And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples that to know him is to know the Father — and from now on they have seen the Father
teachings.quote: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.

teachings.text: The Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins and is Lord of the Sabbath
teachings.quote: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.

teachings.text: All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me
teachings.quote: All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.

teachings.text: Defines his mission — to fulfill the Law, call sinners to repentance, seek the lost, and give his life as ransom
teachings.quote: ¶ Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.

teachings.text: He came to cast fire on the earth — not peace but a sword
teachings.quote: ¶ I am come to send fire on the earth; and what will I, if it be already kindled?

teachings.text: Counters the chief priests' challenge with a question — the baptism of John, whence was it, from heaven or of men?
teachings.quote: I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authority I do these things. The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men?

teachings.text: Refuses to answer when they would not commit — neither tell I you by what authority I do these things
teachings.quote: Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.

teachings.text: Reminds the Pharisees that hungry David ate the showbread reserved for priests
teachings.quote: Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him? How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?

teachings.text: Calls a man with a withered hand forward and asks whether it is lawful to do good or save life on the Sabbath
teachings.quote: Stand forth. Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? Stretch forth thine hand.

teachings.text: He tells his mother at Cana that his hour has not yet come
teachings.quote: Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.

teachings.text: He tells the disciples he has meat to eat that they know nothing of
teachings.quote: I have meat to eat that ye know not of.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples the man's blindness is not from sin but exists so God's works can be revealed in him
teachings.quote: Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him. I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples Lazarus's sickness will not end in death but will glorify God and the Son of God
teachings.quote: This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples Lazarus has fallen asleep and he is going to wake him — those who walk by day do not stumble
teachings.quote: Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world. But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him. Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.

teachings.text: He assures Martha that her brother shall rise again.
teachings.quote: Thy brother shall rise again.

teachings.text: A kingdom divided against itself cannot stand
teachings.quote: Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand? And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges. But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you. Or else how can one enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house. He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.

teachings.text: He descended from heaven and is from above — not of this world; before Abraham was, I AM
teachings.quote: And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man which is in heaven.

teachings.text: He came from the Father into the world and now returns to the Father
teachings.quote: I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.

teachings.text: Came from heaven to do the Father's will — to lose none given to him but raise them up at the last day
teachings.quote: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me. And this is the Father’s will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.

teachings.text: He tells the crowd not to murmur among themselves
teachings.quote: Murmur not among yourselves.

teachings.text: He asks the murmuring disciples whether his teaching offends them, and what if they should see the Son of Man ascend up where he was before
teachings.quote: Doth this offend you? What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?

teachings.text: Tells his brothers his time has not come — the world hates him because he testifies its works are evil
teachings.quote: My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready. The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil. Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.

teachings.text: He cries in the temple that he is not come of himself but is from him who sent him; he knows the Father, but they do not
teachings.quote: Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not. But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.

teachings.text: Foretells he will soon return to the Father — and they cannot follow where he goes
teachings.quote: Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me. Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.

subcategories.title: Predictions of His Suffering, Death, and Resurrection

teachings.text: The Son of Man must suffer many things, be rejected, be killed, and rise on the third day
As Jonah was in the fish three days, so will the Son of Man be in the earth; destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it
teachings.quote: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

teachings.text: As Moses lifted up the serpent, so must the Son of Man be lifted up — and when lifted, will draw all people
teachings.quote: ¶ And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

teachings.text: The good shepherd lays down his life of his own accord; unless a grain of wheat falls and dies, it cannot bear fruit
teachings.quote: Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again. No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.

teachings.text: Everything written about him in the Prophets will be accomplished; the Christ must suffer and rise on the third day
teachings.quote: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man shall be accomplished. For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on: And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.

teachings.text: Rebukes Peter for opposing the cross — 'Get behind me, Satan' — Peter's mind is on man's things, not God's
teachings.quote: Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.

teachings.text: Charges Peter, James, and John to keep the Transfiguration secret until after his resurrection
teachings.quote: Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.

teachings.text: Affirms Elijah has already come and was mistreated — and the Son of Man will suffer the same way
teachings.quote: Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things. But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.

teachings.text: Rebukes Peter for opposing his foretold suffering — 'Get behind me, Satan'
teachings.quote: Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.

teachings.text: Speaks of the baptism of suffering ahead and how distressed he is until it is accomplished
teachings.quote: But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!

teachings.text: Foretells he will be handed to the Gentiles, mocked, scourged, killed — and rise on the third day
teachings.quote: For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on: And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.

teachings.text: He drives out the dove sellers from the temple, declaring that his Father's house must not be made a house of merchandise
teachings.quote: Take these things hence; make not my Father’s house an house of merchandise.

teachings.text: He defends Mary's anointing — she has kept this for the day of his burying; the poor are always with you, but he will not always be present.
teachings.quote: Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this. For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.

teachings.text: Announces that the hour for the Son of Man to be glorified has come
teachings.quote: The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.

teachings.text: Warns them in advance so they will believe — the prince of this world comes but has no claim on him
teachings.quote: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe. Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me. But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.

teachings.text: He declares to the Samaritan woman: I who speak to you am the Messiah
teachings.quote: I that speak unto thee am he.

teachings.text: The works done in the Father's name bear witness — if not doing the Father's works, do not believe
teachings.quote: I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me. If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not. But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.

teachings.text: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the dead are raised, the poor have good news — blessed is he who does not stumble over me
teachings.quote: Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them. And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.

teachings.text: Identifies John the Baptist as the prophesied Elijah for those willing to accept it
teachings.quote: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.

teachings.text: Defines his earthly mission — sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel
teachings.quote: I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

teachings.text: Asks the disciples at Caesarea Philippi — whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
teachings.quote: Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?

teachings.text: Presses the disciples to confess for themselves who they say he is
teachings.quote: But whom say ye that I am?

teachings.text: Blesses Peter for his confession — flesh and blood did not reveal it to him, but the Father in heaven
teachings.quote: Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.

teachings.text: Reassures the disciples after the Transfiguration — arise, and be not afraid
teachings.quote: Arise, and be not afraid.

teachings.text: Teaches Peter that the sons are free from the temple tax, but provides it from a coin in a fish's mouth to avoid offense
teachings.quote: What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers? Then are the children free. Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.

teachings.text: Declares his mission to seek the lost — for the Son of man is come to save that which was lost
teachings.quote: For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.

teachings.text: Sends two disciples to fetch the colt for the triumphal entry — tell the owner the Lord has need of it
teachings.quote: Go into the village over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me. And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.

teachings.text: Silences the Pharisees with a question about Christ — whose son is he? If David in spirit calls him Lord, how is he his son?
teachings.quote: What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool? If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?

teachings.text: Affirms that Elijah comes first to restore all things — and has already come and been mistreated
teachings.quote: Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought. But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.

teachings.text: Defends the disciples' praise at the triumphal entry — if they were silent, even the stones would cry out
teachings.quote: I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.

teachings.text: He greets Nathanael as a true Israelite without guile and reveals he saw him under the fig tree before Philip called
teachings.quote: Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile! Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.

teachings.text: Promises Nathanael will see greater things — heaven open and angels ascending and descending on the Son of Man
teachings.quote: Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.

teachings.text: He asks the Samaritan woman at the well to give him a drink
teachings.quote: Give me to drink.

teachings.text: He tells the woman to call her husband and reveals he knows she has had five husbands and the man she now has is not her husband
teachings.quote: Go, call thy husband, and come hither. Thou hast well said, I have no husband: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.

teachings.text: He reveals himself to the healed man, declaring that the one who talks with him is the Son of God
teachings.quote: Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.

teachings.text: Announces the Son of Man is now glorified and warns the disciples he will be with them only a little while longer
teachings.quote: Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him. If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him. Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.

teachings.text: Defends his disciples by citing David eating the showbread and priests working on the Sabbath — one greater than the temple is here
teachings.quote: Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him; How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests? Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless? But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.

teachings.text: Reads Isaiah 61 in the Nazareth synagogue and declares the scripture fulfilled — the Spirit of the Lord has anointed him; today this is fulfilled in your ears
teachings.quote: This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.

teachings.text: Submits to John's baptism — it is fitting to fulfill all righteousness
teachings.quote: Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness.

teachings.text: Cleanses a leper with a touch and instructs him to show himself to the priest and offer the gift Moses commanded as a testimony
teachings.quote: I will; be thou clean. See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

teachings.text: Responds to the centurion's request for his servant — I will come and heal him
teachings.quote: I will come and heal him.

teachings.text: Authorizes demons to enter the herd of swine with a single word — Go
teachings.quote: Go.

teachings.text: Silences the mourners at Jairus's house declaring the girl is not dead but sleeping, then raises her with the words 'Talitha cumi'
teachings.quote: Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth. ... Talitha cumi;

teachings.text: Charges the healed blind men to silence — see that no man know it
teachings.quote: See that no man know it.

teachings.text: Heals a man's withered hand on the Sabbath
teachings.quote: Stretch forth thine hand.

teachings.text: Calls for the loaves and fishes before feeding the five thousand — bring them hither to me
teachings.quote: Bring them hither to me.

teachings.text: Asks the disciples how many loaves they have before feeding the four thousand
teachings.quote: How many loaves have ye?

teachings.text: Rebukes an unclean spirit in the Capernaum synagogue, commanding it to be silent and come out
teachings.quote: Hold thy peace, and come out of him.

teachings.text: Peace, be still — Jesus rebukes the wind and commands the sea, and there was a great calm
teachings.quote: Peace, be still.

teachings.text: Confronts the Gerasene demoniac, asking the unclean spirit its name — Legion
teachings.quote: Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit. What is thy name?

teachings.text: Perceives that virtue has gone out of him and asks who touched him in the crowd
teachings.quote: Who touched my clothes?

teachings.text: Tells the disciples to feed the crowd themselves and asks how many loaves they have — sets up the feeding of five thousand
teachings.quote: Give ye them to eat. How many loaves have ye? go and see.

teachings.text: Ephphatha — Be opened — Jesus heals the deaf man with an impeded speech impediment
teachings.quote: Ephphatha,

teachings.text: Has compassion on the crowd that has gone three days without food and resolves to feed them before sending them home
teachings.quote: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.

teachings.text: Casts out a deaf and mute spirit from a boy and forbids it to return
teachings.quote: Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.

teachings.text: Asks blind Bartimaeus what he wants and restores his sight, declaring his faith has made him whole
teachings.quote: What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole.

teachings.text: Raises the widow's son at Nain from his bier with a word
teachings.quote: Weep not. Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.

teachings.text: Turns water into wine at Cana — instructs the servants to fill the jars and serve the master of the feast
teachings.quote: Fill the waterpots with water. Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast.

teachings.text: He sends the nobleman home with the word that his son lives, healing the boy at a distance
teachings.quote: Go thy way; thy son liveth.

teachings.text: He commands the lame man to rise, take up his bed, and walk
teachings.quote: Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.

teachings.text: He asks Philip where they shall buy bread to feed the great multitude that has come to him
teachings.quote: Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?

teachings.text: He instructs the disciples to make the men sit down before the feeding of the five thousand
teachings.quote: Make the men sit down.

teachings.text: He tells the disciples to gather up the leftover fragments so that nothing be lost
teachings.quote: Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.

teachings.text: He sends the man born blind to wash in the pool of Siloam, and the man returns seeing
teachings.quote: Go, wash in the pool of Siloam,

teachings.text: At Lazarus's tomb, commands the stone removed, thanks the Father, and calls Lazarus out
teachings.quote: Take ye away the stone. Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God? Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me. And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me. Lazarus, come forth. Loose him, and let him go.

subcategories.title: The Promise and Coming of the Spirit

teachings.text: Promises the Father will send another Helper, the Spirit of truth, to dwell with them forever
teachings.quote: And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever; Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.

teachings.text: The Helper, sent in his name, will teach all things, bring his words to remembrance, and bear witness about him
teachings.quote: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.

teachings.text: Tells them his departure is for their good — only then will the Helper come and guide them into all truth
teachings.quote: Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you. Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come. He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you.

teachings.text: Wait for the promise of the Father — baptized with the Holy Spirit; you will receive power and be his witnesses; receive the Holy Spirit
teachings.quote: but wait for the promise of the Father, which, ye have heard of me. For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.

teachings.text: He cries on the great day of the feast that if any man thirsts, let him come to him and drink
teachings.quote: If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.

teachings.text: Promises not to leave them as orphans — he will come to them, and those who keep his commandments will be loved by the Father
teachings.quote: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you. Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also. At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you. He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.

teachings.text: Peter recalls the word of the Lord: John baptized with water, but you shall be baptized with the Holy Spirit
teachings.quote: John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.

subcategories.title: The Spirit's Work in the World

teachings.text: The Spirit will convict the world of sin (unbelief), righteousness (Jesus's return to the Father), and judgment (the ruler of this world condemned)
teachings.quote: And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment: Of sin, because they believe not on me; Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more; Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.

teachings.text: The Spirit gives life; the flesh profits nothing; out of the believer will flow rivers of living water
teachings.quote: It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.

teachings.text: Assures the disciples the Father gives the Holy Spirit to those who ask
teachings.quote: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?

teachings.text: Tells the disciples there is more to say, but they cannot bear it yet
teachings.quote: I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.

teachings.text: Tells them all the Father has is his, so the Spirit will reveal it — and soon they will see him again
teachings.quote: All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you. A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.

subcategories.title: The Spirit in Witness and Trial

teachings.text: Do not be anxious about what to say when brought before rulers — the Spirit of the Father will speak through you in that hour
teachings.quote: But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak. For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.

teachings.text: Every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven, but blasphemy against the Spirit will not — in this age or the age to come
teachings.quote: ¶ Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men. And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.

categories.title: The Kingdom of God
categories.description: The Kingdom of God (or Kingdom of Heaven) is the organizing center of Jesus’s teaching. He describes its nature, how one enters it, its ethics, and its ultimate completion.

subcategories.title: Nature and Character of the Kingdom

teachings.text: Kingdom of heaven compared to a mustard seed — smallest of seeds grows into a large tree sheltering birds
teachings.quote: The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.

teachings.text: Kingdom of heaven compared to leaven hidden in three measures of flour — permeates the whole
teachings.quote: The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.

teachings.text: Kingdom compared to treasure hidden in a field, and to a merchant who sells everything for one pearl of great value — worth giving up everything
teachings.quote: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field. ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.

teachings.text: Kingdom compared to a net cast into the sea — gathers all kinds, sorted at the end of the age
teachings.quote: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away. So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just, And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

teachings.text: Kingdom compared to a king who gave a wedding banquet — those invited refused, so the invitation went out to all
teachings.quote: The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son, And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come. Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage. But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise: And the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them. But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth: and he sent forth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city. Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy. Go ye therefore into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage. So those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was furnished with guests. ¶ And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment: And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless. Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth. For many are called, but few are chosen.

teachings.text: Kingdom of heaven compared to ten virgins awaiting the bridegroom — be ready
teachings.quote: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom. And five of them were wise, and five were foolish. They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them: But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps. While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept. And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him. Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps. And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out. But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves. And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut. Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us. But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not. Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.

teachings.text: The kingdom does not come with outward observation — it is in your midst / among you
teachings.quote: The kingdom of God cometh not with observation: Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.

teachings.text: Begins his Galilean ministry by proclaiming the kingdom of God is at hand and calling for repentance
teachings.quote: The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.

teachings.text: The kingdom has been forcefully advancing since the days of John; it has been given to those who will produce its fruit
teachings.quote: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.

teachings.text: Many from east and west will come and recline at table in the kingdom
teachings.quote: And I say unto you, That many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.

teachings.text: Likens the trained kingdom scribe to a householder who brings out treasures both new and old
teachings.quote: Have ye understood all these things? Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.

subcategories.title: Growth and Presence of the Kingdom

teachings.text: Seed growing secretly — a man scatters seed; it grows without his effort until harvest
teachings.quote: So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground; And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how. For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear. But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.

teachings.text: The sower — the same seed falls on different soils and produces different responses to the kingdom message
teachings.quote: Behold, a sower went forth to sow; And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away. And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear. ¶ Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower. When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side. But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it; Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended. He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful. But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.

teachings.text: Wheat and tares — kingdom and evil coexist until the final harvest, when angels will separate them
teachings.quote: The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way. But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also. So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares? He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up? But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them. Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn. He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man; The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one; The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels. As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world. The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity; And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth. Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

teachings.text: Explains why he speaks in parables — kingdom mysteries are given to disciples but veiled to outsiders
teachings.quote: Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given. For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath. Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples that understanding the parable of the sower is the key to understanding all his parables
teachings.quote: Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?

teachings.text: Entry requires being born again — of water and Spirit; what is born of flesh is flesh; the Spirit moves like the wind
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God. Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God. That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit. Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again. The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.

teachings.text: Unless righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, entry is impossible
teachings.quote: For I say unto you, That except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.

teachings.text: Unless one becomes like a child, he cannot enter the kingdom
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.

teachings.text: Not everyone who says “Lord, Lord” will enter — only he who does the will of the Father
teachings.quote: ¶ Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven. Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works? And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.

teachings.text: Enter by the narrow gate — broad and easy is the way to destruction; few find the narrow way
teachings.quote: ¶ Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat: Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.

teachings.text: It is hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom — easier for a camel through a needle's eye
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven. And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

teachings.text: Seek first the kingdom and his righteousness — all else will be added; many are called but few are chosen
teachings.quote: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.

teachings.text: The tax collectors and prostitutes enter the kingdom before the religious leaders who refused to believe
teachings.quote: Whether of them twain did the will of his father? Verily I say unto you, That the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you. For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not: but the publicans and the harlots believed him: and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterward, that ye might believe him.

teachings.text: Quotes Isaiah's prophecy fulfilled in dull-hearted hearers — they hear but understand not, see but perceive not, lest they be converted
teachings.quote: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive: For this people’s heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

subcategories.title: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes

teachings.text: Blessed are the poor in spirit — theirs is the kingdom of heaven
teachings.quote: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

teachings.text: Blessed are those who mourn — they shall be comforted
teachings.quote: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.

teachings.text: Blessed are the meek — they shall inherit the earth
teachings.quote: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.

teachings.text: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness — they shall be satisfied
teachings.quote: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.

teachings.text: Blessed are the merciful — they shall receive mercy
teachings.quote: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.

teachings.text: Blessed are the pure in heart — they shall see God
teachings.quote: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.

teachings.text: Blessed are the peacemakers — they shall be called sons of God
teachings.quote: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.

teachings.text: Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness — theirs is the kingdom; rejoice, great is the reward in heaven
teachings.quote: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake. Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.

teachings.text: The parable of the two sons — one says no but goes, one says yes but does not go
teachings.quote: ¶ But what think ye? A certain man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, Son, go work to day in my vineyard. He answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went. And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir: and went not. Whether of them twain did the will of his father? Verily I say unto you, That the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.

teachings.text: The parable of the vineyard workers — all receive the same wage regardless of hours; the last shall be first
teachings.quote: For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard. And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard. And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace, And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way. Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise. And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle? They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive. So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first. And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny. But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny. And when they had received it, they murmured against the goodman of the house, Saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day. But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny? Take that thine is, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee. Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good? So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen.

teachings.text: The parable of the wicked tenants — the vineyard taken from those who killed the owner's son
teachings.quote: ¶ Hear another parable: There was a certain householder, which planted a vineyard, and hedged it round about, and digged a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country: And when the time of the fruit drew near, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, that they might receive the fruits of it. And the husbandmen took his servants, and beat one, and killed another, and stoned another. Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they did unto them likewise. But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son. But when the husbandmen saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and let us seize on his inheritance. And they caught him, and cast him out of the vineyard, and slew him. When the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh, what will he do unto those husbandmen? Did ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner: this is the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes? Therefore say I unto you, The kingdom of God shall be taken from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof. And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.

teachings.text: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand — the time is fulfilled
teachings.quote: Repent: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.

teachings.text: He has come to call sinners to repentance, not the righteous; unless you repent you will all likewise perish
teachings.quote: They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick. But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

teachings.text: There is more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than ninety-nine who need no repentance
teachings.quote: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance. Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.

teachings.text: Parable of the barren fig tree — the owner gives one final year to bear fruit before judgment falls
teachings.quote: A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit thereon, and found none. Then said he unto the dresser of his vineyard, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why cumbereth it the ground? And he answering said unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung it: And if it bear fruit, well: and if not, then after that thou shalt cut it down.

teachings.text: Pronounces woe on Chorazin and Bethsaida — Tyre and Sidon would have repented in sackcloth and ashes had they seen such mighty works
teachings.quote: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

teachings.text: Asks whether the Galileans Pilate killed were greater sinners — calls all to repent or likewise perish
teachings.quote: Suppose ye that these Galilæans were sinners above all the Galilæans, because they suffered such things?

teachings.text: Asks whether the eighteen killed when the tower in Siloam fell were greater sinners than other Jerusalemites
teachings.quote: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?

teachings.text: Calls Zacchaeus down from the sycamore tree — he must stay at his house today
teachings.quote: Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.

teachings.text: Declares salvation has come to Zacchaeus's house — he too is a son of Abraham
teachings.quote: This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.

teachings.text: He finds the healed man in the temple and warns him to sin no more lest a worse thing come upon him
teachings.quote: Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.

teachings.text: Warns that an unclean spirit returning with seven worse spirits leaves the person's final state worse than the first
teachings.quote: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none. Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished. Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.

teachings.text: He asks the woman where her accusers are and tells her that neither does he condemn her — go and sin no more
teachings.quote: Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee? Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.

teachings.text: Lost sheep — a shepherd leaves ninety-nine to search for the one; rejoices when found
teachings.quote: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it? And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing. And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost. I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.

teachings.text: Lost coin — a woman searches the whole house; calls her neighbors when found
teachings.quote: ¶ Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it? And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost. Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.

teachings.text: Lost son — a son returns from ruin; his father runs to meet him and restores him fully
teachings.quote: A certain man had two sons: And the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living. And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living. And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want. And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine. And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him. And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger! I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee, And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants. And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him. And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son. But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry. Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing. And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant. And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound. And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him. And he answering said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf. And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine. It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.

teachings.text: Come to me, all who are weary and burdened — take my yoke and learn from me; I am gentle and lowly; my yoke is easy and burden light
teachings.quote: ¶ Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.

teachings.text: Promises that all whom the Father gives him will come to him, and whoever comes he will never cast out
teachings.quote: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.

teachings.text: Entry into the kingdom requires being born again — of water and Spirit; flesh births flesh, Spirit births spirit
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God. Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God. That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

teachings.text: The Spirit moves like the wind — its origin and destination unseen; so it is with everyone born of the Spirit
teachings.quote: Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again. The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.

teachings.text: Rebukes Nicodemus, a teacher of Israel, for not understanding — if earthly things baffle, how can heavenly things be received?
teachings.quote: Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness. If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?

teachings.text: Identifies the unbelief in their midst — no one can come to him unless granted by the Father
teachings.quote: But there are some of you that believe not. Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.

subcategories.title: Faith as the Condition of Salvation

teachings.text: Whoever believes in the Son shall not perish but have eternal life and will not be judged; whoever rejects him will not see life
teachings.quote: ¶ For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life. For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved. ¶ He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.

teachings.text: I am the way, the truth, and the life — no one comes to the Father except through me
teachings.quote: I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.

teachings.text: I am the resurrection and the life — whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live; whoever lives and believes shall never die
teachings.quote: I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?

teachings.text: Urges the crowd to walk and believe while the light is with them, so they may become children of light
teachings.quote: Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth. While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light.

subcategories.title: Eternal Life — Its Nature and Assurance

teachings.text: My sheep hear my voice — I know them, they follow me, I give them eternal life; no one can snatch them from my hand
teachings.quote: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.

teachings.text: Promises that the water he gives will become a spring of water welling up to eternal life — those who drink it will never thirst again
teachings.quote: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.

teachings.text: In my Father's house are many rooms — he goes to prepare a place and will come again to receive his own
teachings.quote: In my Father’s house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you. And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.

teachings.text: Rebukes the crowd for chasing him after the loaves — work not for perishable food but for food that lasts to eternal life
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled. Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.

teachings.text: All taught by the Father come to him — only the one from God has seen the Father, and whoever believes has eternal life
teachings.quote: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me. Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father. Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.

teachings.text: Whoever commits sin is the servant of sin, and the servant does not abide in the house forever, but the Son abides forever
teachings.quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin. And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.

teachings.text: He has no devil but honors his Father; he seeks not his own glory, and if any man keeps his saying, he shall never see death
teachings.quote: I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me. And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth. Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.

teachings.text: Contrasts the thief who comes to destroy with himself — he came so they may have life abundantly
teachings.quote: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.

teachings.text: Enter by the narrow gate — broad and easy is the way to destruction; the narrow way leads to life and few find it
teachings.quote: ¶ Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat: Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.

teachings.text: Not everyone who says “Lord, Lord” will enter — only he who does the will of the Father; many are called, few chosen
teachings.quote: ¶ Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven. Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works? And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.

teachings.text: Warns that once the master shuts the door, those who only knew him casually will be told 'I never knew you'
teachings.quote: When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are: Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets. But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity.

teachings.text: Calls Herod a fox and laments over Jerusalem — how often he longed to gather her children, but they refused
teachings.quote: Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third day I shall be perfected. Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the day following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.
