And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave them to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee. Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves? Go, and do thou likewise.

teachings.text: You neglect justice and the love of God — these ought to have been practiced without neglecting the others
teachings.quote: But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

teachings.text: Has compassion on the crowd that has gone three days without food and refuses to send them away hungry
teachings.quote: I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.

teachings.text: Stops for the two blind men outside Jericho and asks what they want him to do
teachings.quote: What will ye that I shall do unto you?

teachings.text: Sends ten lepers to show themselves to the priests — they are cleansed as they go
teachings.quote: Go shew yourselves unto the priests.

teachings.text: Notes that only the Samaritan among the ten cleansed lepers returned to give glory to God
teachings.quote: Were there not ten cleansed? but where are the nine? There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.

teachings.text: Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men — come, follow me
teachings.quote: Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.

teachings.text: Foxes have holes and birds have nests — the Son of Man has no place to lay his head; let the dead bury their own dead
teachings.quote: The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head. Follow me; and let the dead bury their dead.

teachings.text: No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom
teachings.quote: No man, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.

teachings.text: Tells the anxious Martha that Mary has chosen the one needful thing, and it will not be taken from her
teachings.quote: Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things: But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her.

teachings.text: Calls Matthew the tax collector from his booth — Follow me
teachings.quote: Follow me.

teachings.text: Redefines family — who is my mother and brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will
teachings.quote: Who is my mother? and who are my brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren!

teachings.text: Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the tax collector to leave his post and become a disciple
teachings.quote: Follow me.

teachings.text: Calls the returning apostles to withdraw with him to a quiet place and rest
teachings.quote: Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while:

teachings.text: Tells Peter to put out into deep water and let down the nets for the miraculous catch
teachings.quote: Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.

teachings.text: Calls Peter from his nets — from now on he will catch people
teachings.quote: Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.

teachings.text: Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the publican from the receipt of custom
teachings.quote: Follow me.

teachings.text: He asks the first disciples what they seek and invites them to come and see where he dwells
teachings.quote: What seek ye? Come and see.

teachings.text: He renames Simon son of Jonah as Cephas, meaning a stone, marking him with a new identity
teachings.quote: Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas,

teachings.text: He reminds the twelve that he has chosen them, yet one of them is a devil
teachings.quote: Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?

teachings.text: He calls the disciples to go back into Judaea again despite the danger awaiting him there.
teachings.quote: Let us go into Judæa again.

teachings.text: Where he goes Peter cannot follow now, but shall follow afterwards.
teachings.quote: Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.

teachings.text: Three times asks Peter "Do you love me?" — feed my lambs, tend my sheep, feed my sheep; predicts his martyrdom and calls him to follow
teachings.quote: Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? Feed my lambs... Feed my sheep. Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself... but when thou shalt be old... another shall gird thee... Follow me.

teachings.text: Anyone who would follow must deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow — the cost is total self-surrender
teachings.quote: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

teachings.text: Whoever saves his life will lose it — whoever loses it for his sake will find it; what profits a man to gain the world and forfeit his soul?
teachings.quote: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it. For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

teachings.text: Anyone who does not renounce family, possessions, and even his own life cannot be a disciple
teachings.quote: If any man come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple. So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.

teachings.text: The tower builder and the king going to war — count the cost before you begin
teachings.quote: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it? Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that behold it begin to mock him, Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish. Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand? Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace. So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.

teachings.text: Confronts shallow disciples — why call him Lord but ignore his commands?
teachings.quote: ¶ And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?

teachings.text: Rebukes James and John for wanting to call fire on the Samaritans — he came to save lives, not destroy them
teachings.quote: Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of. For the Son of man is not come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.

teachings.text: He asks the twelve whether they also will go away
teachings.quote: Will ye also go away?

teachings.text: Defines true discipleship as continuing in his word
teachings.quote: If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;

teachings.text: Those who leave family or possessions for the kingdom will receive many times more, with eternal life
teachings.quote: And every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life.

teachings.text: A good tree bears good fruit; a bad tree cannot — by their fruits you shall know them; every fruitless tree is cut down
teachings.quote: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit. A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit. Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire. Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.

teachings.text: He who abides in him bears much fruit — apart from him you can do nothing; the Father is glorified by much fruit
teachings.quote: I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing. Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.

teachings.text: He chose them to go and bear fruit that abides; if his words abide in them, they may ask whatever they wish
teachings.quote: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you. Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.

teachings.text: Teaches that those who lose their life for him keep it — and even with a troubled soul, he chose this hour to glorify the Father
teachings.quote: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal. If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour. Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour. Father, glorify thy name.

teachings.text: Promises that those who endure to the end will be saved
teachings.quote: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.

teachings.text: Answers Satan's first temptation by quoting Deuteronomy — man does not live by bread alone
teachings.quote: It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.

teachings.text: Refuses to test God by quoting Deuteronomy — do not put the Lord your God to the test
teachings.quote: It is written again, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

teachings.text: Commands Satan to leave — worship the Lord your God and serve him only
teachings.quote: Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

teachings.text: Answers the bread temptation in Luke's account by citing Deuteronomy
teachings.quote: It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.

teachings.text: Rebukes Satan and quotes the command to worship and serve God alone
teachings.quote: Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

teachings.text: Refuses the temple-pinnacle temptation — do not put the Lord your God to the test
teachings.quote: It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

teachings.text: Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness — blessed when people revile and persecute; rejoice, for great is the reward in heaven
teachings.quote: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake. Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.

teachings.text: If they persecuted him, they will persecute his followers — a servant is not greater than his master; you will be hated by all for his name
teachings.quote: Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.

teachings.text: They will be put out of synagogues; the hour is coming when killing them will seem like worship of God
teachings.quote: They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.

teachings.text: Notes the rejection at Nazareth — a prophet is honored everywhere except his hometown
teachings.quote: A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.

teachings.text: Notes that a prophet is dishonored only by his hometown, his relatives, and his own household
teachings.quote: A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.

teachings.text: If the world hates them they should know it hated him first; because they are not of the world but chosen out of it, the world hates them.
teachings.quote: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you. If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples the world will persecute them because it has rejected him and the Father — fulfilling the scripture 'they hated me without cause'
teachings.quote: But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me. If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin. He that hateth me hateth my Father also. If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father. But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.

teachings.text: Do not fear those who kill the body but not the soul — fear him who can destroy both; not a hair of your head will perish
teachings.quote: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.

teachings.text: In the world they will have tribulation — but take heart; he has overcome the world; by endurance they will gain their lives
teachings.quote: These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.

teachings.text: Answers Paul's plea about the thorn — his grace is sufficient and his strength is perfected in weakness
teachings.quote: My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.

teachings.text: Has warned the disciples in advance so they will not stumble when persecution comes
teachings.quote: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.

teachings.text: Forewarns the disciples of persecution — sorrow fills their hearts now, but he is going to the Father who sent him
teachings.quote: And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me. But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you. But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou? But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

teachings.text: Promises their grief will turn to joy like a mother's after birth — a joy no one will take from them
teachings.quote: Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me? Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy. A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world. And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.

teachings.text: Warns the disciples they will scatter and abandon him — yet he is not alone because the Father is with him
teachings.quote: Do ye now believe? Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

teachings.text: Anyone who would follow must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow — whoever saves his life will lose it; whoever loses it for his sake will save it
teachings.quote: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me. For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it. Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.

teachings.text: What does it profit a man to gain the whole world and forfeit his soul?
teachings.quote: For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

subcategories.title: The Seven Woes to Scribes and Pharisees (Matthew 23)

teachings.text: Woe: they shut the kingdom of heaven in people's faces — they neither enter nor let others in
teachings.quote: ¶ But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye neither go in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in.

teachings.text: Woe: they travel far to make one convert, then make him twice the child of hell
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourselves.

teachings.text: Woe: blind guides who strain out a gnat but swallow a camel — neglecting what actually sanctifies
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, ye blind guides, which say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor! Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold? And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it, he is guilty. Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift? Whoso therefore shall swear by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon. And whoso shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein. And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon. Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone. Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.

teachings.text: Woe: they tithe mint and dill but neglect justice, mercy, and faithfulness
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

teachings.text: Woe: they clean the outside of the cup while inside is greed and self-indulgence
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and excess. Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may be clean also.

teachings.text: Woe: they are like whitewashed tombs — beautiful outside, full of dead bones within
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men’s bones, and of all uncleanness. Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.

teachings.text: Woe: they build tombs for prophets while being sons of those who killed them; brood of vipers — how will they escape hell?
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous, And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets. Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets. Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers. Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?

teachings.text: They devour widows' houses and make long prayers for a show
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayer: therefore ye shall receive the greater damnation.

teachings.text: Pronounces woe on the Pharisees who crave the best seats and respectful greetings in public
teachings.quote: Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.

teachings.text: Pronounces woe on the lawyers who pile heavy burdens on others but won't lift a finger to carry them
teachings.quote: Woe unto you also, ye lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.

teachings.text: Pronounces woe on lawyers who take away the key of knowledge — blocking the way and refusing to enter themselves
teachings.quote: Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and some of them they shall slay and persecute: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation; From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation. Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.

teachings.text: These people honor him with their lips but their hearts are far from him — in vain they worship, teaching human traditions
teachings.quote: This people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth, and honoureth me with their lips; but their heart is far from me. But in vain they do worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.

teachings.text: It is not what goes into a person that defiles him — what defiles comes from within: evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, slander, arrogance
teachings.quote: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man. But those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man. For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:

teachings.text: The Pharisee and the tax collector — self-righteousness condemned; humility justified
teachings.quote: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican. The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican. I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess. And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as his eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner. I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

teachings.text: Compares the fickle generation to children in the marketplace — they rejected both John's fasting and his own feasting
teachings.quote: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows, And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented. For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil. The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.

teachings.text: Refuses the sign-seekers — only the sign of Jonah will be given to this evil generation
teachings.quote: An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:

teachings.text: Repeats the rebuke — only the sign of Jonah will be given to this wicked generation
teachings.quote: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas.

teachings.text: Curses the fruitless fig tree — it will never bear fruit again
teachings.quote: Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever.

teachings.text: Tests the Syrophoenician woman — the children's bread is not for the dogs
teachings.quote: Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.

subcategories.title: Traditions of Men Versus God's Commands

teachings.text: They reject God's commands to keep their own traditions — the tradition of Corban nullifies the command to honor parents
teachings.quote: Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition. For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free. And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother; Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.

teachings.text: They have made void the word of God for the sake of tradition — Isaiah prophesied rightly about them
teachings.quote: And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition. Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,

teachings.text: Accuses the Pharisees of setting aside God's command to keep human traditions like ritual washings
teachings.quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.

teachings.text: Calls the crowd to listen carefully as he teaches what truly defiles a person
teachings.quote: Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:

teachings.text: Explains to the disciples that what enters from outside cannot defile — defilement comes from within
teachings.quote: Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him; Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats? That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.

teachings.text: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man — the heart is the source of moral corruption
teachings.quote: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.

teachings.text: The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath — the Son of Man is Lord of it
teachings.quote: The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.

teachings.text: It is lawful to do good on the Sabbath — would you not rescue an animal or a child? This woman bound by Satan ought to be loosed
teachings.quote: What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out? How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.

teachings.text: Reminds the Pharisees that hungry David ate the showbread which only priests were permitted
teachings.quote: Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him; How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?

teachings.text: Calls the man with the withered hand forward and asks if it is lawful to do good or save life on the Sabbath
teachings.quote: Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it? Stretch forth thy hand.

teachings.text: Heals the bent-over woman in the synagogue — releases her from eighteen years of infirmity
teachings.quote: Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.

teachings.text: Calls the synagogue ruler a hypocrite — they all untie an ox or donkey to water it on the Sabbath
teachings.quote: Thou hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall, and lead him away to watering?

teachings.text: Asks the lawyers and Pharisees whether Sabbath healing is lawful before healing the man with dropsy
teachings.quote: Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?

teachings.text: He defends his Sabbath healing by appealing to the lawful practice of circumcision on the Sabbath, asking why they are angry that he made a man whole
teachings.quote: I have done one work, and ye all marvel. Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man. If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?

teachings.text: The scribes and Pharisees sit on Moses's seat — do what they say but not what they do; they burden others but will not lift a finger
teachings.quote: The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat: All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works: for they say, and do not. For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.

teachings.text: They do all their deeds to be seen — they love places of honor, greetings in marketplaces, and titles
teachings.quote: But all their works they do for to be seen of men: they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments, And love the uppermost rooms at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues, And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi. But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your Master, even Christ; and all ye are brethren. Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, even Christ. ¶ Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from city to city: That upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the temple and the altar. Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation. O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not! Behold, your house is left unto you desolate. For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

teachings.text: Warns the disciples that every plant the Father did not plant will be uprooted
teachings.quote: Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.

teachings.text: Concludes the teaching on defilement — these are the things that defile a man, but eating with unwashen hands does not
teachings.quote: These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.

teachings.text: Cleanses the temple — declares it the house of prayer that they have made into a robbers' den
teachings.quote: It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.

teachings.text: Curses the unfruitful fig tree as a sign against barren, pretentious religion
teachings.quote: No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever.

teachings.text: Cleanses the temple, citing Isaiah — God's house for all nations has been turned into a robbers' den
teachings.quote: Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.

teachings.text: Counters the authority challenge with his own question — was John's baptism from heaven or from men?
teachings.quote: I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things. The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.

teachings.text: Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things — Jesus refuses the chief priests' challenge
teachings.quote: Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

teachings.text: Exposes the Pharisees who justify themselves before men — what they prize, God despises
teachings.quote: Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.

teachings.text: Cleanses the temple in Luke's account — God's house of prayer turned into a robbers' den
teachings.quote: It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.

teachings.text: Exposes that they reject him while seeking human glory — and Moses, whom they trust, accuses them
teachings.quote: I receive not honour from men. But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you. I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive. How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only? Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.

teachings.text: Tells the religious leaders their father is the devil — a murderer and liar — not Abraham, since they reject his truth
teachings.quote: I know that ye are Abraham’s seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word hath no place in you. I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father. If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham. But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham. Ye do the deeds of your father. If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me. Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word. Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it. And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not. Which of you convinceth me of sin? And if I say the truth, why do ye not believe me? He that is of God heareth God’s words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.

teachings.text: Declares he sends prophets and wise men whom they will kill — pronounces the Jerusalem lament: how often would he have gathered her children, but they were not willing
teachings.quote: Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify... O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!

teachings.text: On this rock he will build his church — the gates of hell shall not prevail; he gives the keys of the kingdom
teachings.quote: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it. And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.

teachings.text: Grants the disciples authority to bind and loose — heaven will ratify their decisions
teachings.quote: And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.

teachings.text: If your brother sins, go privately — if he listens, you have won him; take witnesses; then tell the church; treat the unrepentant as an outsider
teachings.quote: ¶ Moreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee, go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone: if he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother. But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established. And if he shall neglect to hear them, tell it unto the church: but if he neglect to hear the church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.

teachings.text: Where two or three are gathered in his name, there he is among them; if two agree about anything they ask, it will be done
teachings.quote: Again I say unto you, That if two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in heaven. For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples not to stop the unknown exorcist working in his name — whoever is not against us is for us
teachings.quote: Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me. For he that is not against us is on our part.

teachings.text: Commissions the eleven, and all followers of Christ, to make disciples of all nations
teachings.quote: All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth. ¶ Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.

teachings.text: You will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you — be my witnesses in Jerusalem, Judaea, Samaria, and to the ends of the earth
teachings.quote: But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.

subcategories.title: Instructions for the Twelve and the Seventy

teachings.text: Go and proclaim: the kingdom of heaven is at hand; heal the sick, raise the dead, cast out demons — freely give
teachings.quote: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand. Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.

teachings.text: Take no bag, no purse — the worker deserves his wages; if a town welcomes, stay; if not, shake the dust off your feet
teachings.quote: Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel. Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses, Nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat. And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, enquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence. And when ye come into an house, salute it. And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you. And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet. But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues; It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household? He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it. ¶ He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me. He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward. And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.

teachings.text: Behold, he sends them as sheep among wolves — be wise as serpents and innocent as doves
teachings.quote: ¶ Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples to move on to neighboring towns — preaching is the purpose of his coming
teachings.quote: Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.

teachings.text: Receives the seventy back joyfully — saw Satan fall like lightning, but they should rejoice their names are in heaven
teachings.quote: I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven. Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you. Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven. I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.

subcategories.title: Visions and Commissions to the Apostles

teachings.text: In a vision sends Ananias to Saul — a chosen instrument to carry his name before Gentiles, kings, and Israel
teachings.quote: Ananias. Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and enquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prayeth, And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight. Go thy way: for he is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel: For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name’s sake.

teachings.text: Appears to Paul in Corinth — speak, do not fear, for many in this city belong to him and no one will harm him
teachings.quote: Be not afraid, but speak, and hold not thy peace: For I am with thee, and no man shall set on thee to hurt thee: for I have much people in this city.

teachings.text: Tells Paul in a temple trance to leave Jerusalem — they will not receive his testimony; he is sent to Gentiles far off
teachings.quote: Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me. Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.

teachings.text: The Lord stands by Paul in his Jerusalem custody and encourages him, confirming that just as he has testified about him in Jerusalem he must also testify in Rome.
teachings.quote: Be of good cheer, Paul: for as thou hast testified of me in Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome.

teachings.text: Tells Peter in a vision to rise, kill, and eat — what God has made clean must not be called common
Confronts Saul on the Damascus road — 'I am Jesus whom you persecute' — and sends him into the city for further instructions
teachings.quote: Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks. Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.

teachings.text: The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few — pray earnestly to the Lord to send out laborers
teachings.quote: The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few; Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.

teachings.text: No prophet is accepted in his own country — Elijah was sent to a widow of Sidon, and only Naaman the Syrian was cleansed
teachings.quote: Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country. Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country. But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land; But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow. And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.

teachings.text: Tells the crowd at Capernaum he must move on to preach the kingdom in other towns
teachings.quote: I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.

teachings.text: Likens the fickle generation to children in the market — they reject both John's austerity and his own table fellowship
teachings.quote: Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like? They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept. For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil. The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners! But wisdom is justified of all her children.

teachings.text: Sends the healed Gerasene demoniac home to tell what God has done for him
teachings.quote: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples his food is to do the Father's will — the fields are ripe for harvest, sower and reaper rejoicing together
teachings.quote: My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work. Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest. And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together. And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth. I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples they will bear witness — they have been with him from the beginning
teachings.quote: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.

teachings.text: What is whispered, proclaim on the housetops; do not fear those who kill the body — fear him who can destroy both soul and body
teachings.quote: What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops. And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.

teachings.text: Everyone who acknowledges him before men, he will acknowledge before the Father; whoever denies him, he will deny
teachings.quote: Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven. But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.

teachings.text: They will be dragged before governors and kings — the gospel must first be proclaimed to all nations
teachings.quote: And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.

teachings.text: Brother will betray brother; they will be hated by all for his name's sake — he who endures to the end will be saved
teachings.quote: And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death. And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.

teachings.text: Not a hair of your head will perish — by your endurance you will gain your lives
teachings.quote: But there shall not an hair of your head perish. In your patience possess ye your souls.

categories.title: The Passion Narrative
categories.description: The words of Jesus during his final hours — the Last Supper, Gethsemane, his trial, and crucifixion.

teachings.text: Foretells his crucifixion at Passover — after two days is the feast, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified
teachings.quote: Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

teachings.text: Defends the woman who anointed him — she has done a good work, pouring ointment for my burial, and shall be remembered wherever the gospel is preached
teachings.quote: Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me. For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always. For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial. Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.

teachings.text: Sends two disciples to prepare the Passover meal at the appointed house
teachings.quote: Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.

teachings.text: Announces at the Last Supper that one of the Twelve will betray him
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.

teachings.text: Identifies the betrayer as the one who dips with him — better for that man if he had never been born
teachings.quote: He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me. The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born. Thou hast said.

teachings.text: At the Last Supper Jesus institutes the bread and the cup — this is my body, this is my blood of the new testament shed for many for the remission of sins; he will not drink of the vine again until the kingdom of his Father.
teachings.quote: Take, eat; this is my body. Drink ye all of it; For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins. But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.

teachings.text: Foretells the disciples' scattering, citing Zechariah — but pledges to meet them in Galilee after he rises
teachings.quote: All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad. But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

teachings.text: Foretells Peter's three denials before the rooster crows that very night
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.

teachings.text: At the Last Supper Jesus institutes the bread and the cup — this is my body, this is my blood of the new testament shed for many; he will not drink of the vine again until that day in the kingdom of God.
teachings.quote: Take, eat: this is my body. This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many. Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.

teachings.text: At the Last Supper Jesus takes the cup, gives thanks, and says he will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes; then he gives the bread — this is my body given for you — and the cup, the new testament in his blood shed for them.
teachings.quote: Take this, and divide it among yourselves: For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come. This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me. This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.

teachings.text: Tells the disciples he longed to share this Passover before his suffering — and will not eat it again until the kingdom comes
teachings.quote: With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer: For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.

teachings.text: At the Last Supper dispute over greatness, tells the disciples that gentile rulers lord over their subjects but among them the greatest must be as the youngest and the leader as one who serves
teachings.quote: And he said unto them, The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors. But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.

teachings.text: At table, asks who is greater — the one who reclines or the one who serves — and declares himself to be among them as one who serves
teachings.quote: For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.

teachings.text: At the Last Supper, commends the disciples for remaining with him through his trials and promises them thrones to judge the twelve tribes; warns Peter that Satan has asked to sift him but that he has prayed for Peter's faith to hold
teachings.quote: Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations. And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me; That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel. And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat: But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.

teachings.text: Greets Judas at the betrayal — friend, do what you came for
teachings.quote: Friend, wherefore art thou come?

teachings.text: Stops Peter's sword at his arrest — those who live by the sword die by it; the Scriptures must be fulfilled
teachings.quote: Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword. Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels? But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be? Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me. But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.

teachings.text: Confronts the arresting party — they come with swords as for a robber, though he taught openly daily in the temple
teachings.quote: Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me? I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.

teachings.text: Confronts Judas at his arrest — does he betray the Son of Man with a kiss?
teachings.quote: Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?

teachings.text: Going forth to those who came to arrest him, he asks, Whom do you seek?
teachings.quote: Whom seek ye?

teachings.text: At his arrest, twice declares 'I am he' — and the arresting party falls back to the ground at the words
teachings.quote: I am he. Whom seek ye? I have told you that I am he: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:

teachings.text: Calls the disciples in Gethsemane to stay alert and pray — the spirit is willing but the flesh is weak
teachings.quote: Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder. My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and watch with me. O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt. What, could ye not watch with me one hour? Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak. O my Father, if this cup may not pass away from me, except I drink it, thy will be done. Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners. Rise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.

teachings.text: Affirms before Pilate that he is the King of the Jews
teachings.quote: Thou sayest.

teachings.text: Tells the weeping women on the way to the cross to weep for themselves and their children, not for him
teachings.quote: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children. For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck. Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us. For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?

teachings.text: Tells the high priest he taught openly in synagogue and temple — those who heard him should be questioned
teachings.quote: I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing. Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.

teachings.text: If he has spoken evil, let the officer bear witness of the evil; but if he has spoken well, why is he struck?
teachings.quote: If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?

teachings.text: He asks Pilate whether he says this of himself, or whether others have told him concerning Jesus.
teachings.quote: Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?

teachings.text: Pilate would have no power against him at all unless it were given from above; therefore the one who delivered him to Pilate has the greater sin.
teachings.quote: Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.

teachings.text: He confirms to the high priest: I AM the Christ, the Son of God, coming on the clouds in power
teachings.quote: Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.

teachings.text: Tells Pilate his kingdom is not of this world — otherwise his servants would fight to prevent his arrest
teachings.quote: My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.

teachings.text: Before Pilate, declares he was born and came into the world to bear witness to the truth — everyone on the side of truth listens to him
teachings.quote: Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.

teachings.text: From the cross Jesus prays for those who are crucifying him, asking the Father to forgive them on the ground that they do not understand what they are doing — extending mercy to his executioners at the very moment of his suffering.
teachings.quote: Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.

teachings.text: Jesus assures the repentant criminal crucified beside him that on that very day he will be with him in paradise, offering immediate salvation to one who turns to him in his final moments.
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.

teachings.text: From the cross Jesus entrusts his mother to the care of the beloved disciple, saying to her 'Woman, behold your son' and to the disciple 'Behold your mother,' establishing a new family bond at the moment of his death.
teachings.quote: Woman, behold thy son! Behold thy mother!

teachings.text: At the ninth hour Jesus cries out with a loud voice the opening words of Psalm 22 — 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' — giving voice to the full weight of divine abandonment he bears as the sin-bearer on the cross.
teachings.quote: Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?

teachings.text: Jesus declares 'I thirst' from the cross, fulfilling Scripture and expressing the full physical humanity of the Son of God in his atoning suffering.
teachings.quote: I thirst.

teachings.text: Jesus cries out 'It is finished' (tetelestai) as his final public word before death, declaring the complete accomplishment of his atoning work and the full discharge of every obligation his mission required.
teachings.quote: It is finished:

teachings.text: With a loud voice Jesus entrusts his spirit to the Father in the words of Psalm 31:5, committing himself to God's care as he breathes his last and demonstrating that his death is a voluntary, trusting surrender into the Father's hands.
teachings.quote: Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit:

teachings.text: Calms the disciples by showing his hands and feet — a spirit has no flesh, and Moses, Prophets, and Psalms speak of him
teachings.quote: Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts? Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have. These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.

teachings.text: Commissions the disciples — repentance and forgiveness will be preached to all nations starting in Jerusalem
teachings.quote: Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem. And ye are witnesses of these things.

teachings.text: Peace be with you — as the Father has sent him, so he sends them; receive the Holy Spirit; those whose sins you forgive are forgiven
teachings.quote: Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? Mary. Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God. Peace be unto you. Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you. Receive ye the Holy Ghost: Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.

teachings.text: Joins the two disciples on the Emmaus road and asks what they are discussing so sadly
teachings.quote: What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?

teachings.text: What things? — Jesus draws out the Emmaus disciples' account of Jesus of Nazareth
teachings.quote: What things?

teachings.text: Rebukes the Emmaus disciples for being slow to believe the prophets — the Christ had to suffer to enter glory
teachings.quote: O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?

teachings.text: Greets the gathered disciples on resurrection evening — peace to you
teachings.quote: Peace be unto you.

teachings.text: Asks the disciples for food and eats fish to confirm his bodily resurrection
teachings.quote: Have ye here any meat?

teachings.text: From the shore he asks the fishermen if they have any meat, and tells them to cast the net on the right side of the ship, where they will find a catch.
teachings.quote: Children, have ye any meat? Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find.

teachings.text: He instructs them to bring of the fish which they have just caught.
teachings.quote: Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.

teachings.text: He invites the disciples, Come and dine.
teachings.quote: Come and dine.

teachings.text: Put your finger here, see his hands, reach into his side — do not disbelieve but believe; blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe
teachings.quote: Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing. Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.

teachings.text: Returns eight days later with Thomas present and greets the disciples with peace
teachings.quote: Peace be unto you.

teachings.text: Wait in Jerusalem for the promised Spirit — not to know times and seasons, but to receive power and be his witnesses to the ends of the earth
teachings.quote: but wait for the promise of the Father, which, ye have heard of me. For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence. It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power. But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.

categories.title: Eschatology and the End Times
categories.description: The Olivet Discourse (Matthew 24–25, Mark 13, Luke 21) is the primary source for this category.

teachings.text: Watch out that no one deceives you — many will come claiming to be the Christ; do not be led astray
teachings.quote: Take heed that no man deceive you. For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many. And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another. And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many. And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.

teachings.text: Wars, famines, earthquakes, and signs in the heavens — the beginning of birth pains, not yet the end
teachings.quote: And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet. For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places. All these are the beginning of sorrows.

teachings.text: The gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world — then the end will come
teachings.quote: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.

teachings.text: The abomination of desolation in the holy place — let those in Judea flee; then great tribulation such as has never been
teachings.quote: When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place, (whoso readeth, let him understand:) Then let them which be in Judaea flee into the mountains: Let him which is on the housetop not come down to take any thing out of his house: Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes. And woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days! But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be. And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened.

teachings.text: Warns that false messiahs and prophets will perform great signs to deceive even the elect
teachings.quote: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.

teachings.text: The sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall — the sign of the Son of Man will appear
teachings.quote: ¶ Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.

teachings.text: Foretells the temple's destruction — not one stone will be left on another
teachings.quote: See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

teachings.text: Foretells the temple's destruction in Mark — every great stone of the buildings will be thrown down
teachings.quote: Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

teachings.text: Weeps over Jerusalem — she missed the time of her visitation, and her enemies will leave nothing standing
teachings.quote: If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes. For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side, And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.

teachings.text: Foretells the temple's destruction in Luke — the days are coming when not one stone will remain
teachings.quote: As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

subcategories.title: The Return of the Son of Man

teachings.text: The Son of Man will come on the clouds with power and great glory — his angels will gather the elect from the four winds
teachings.quote: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory. And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.

teachings.text: As lightning flashes from east to west, so will his coming be — as sudden and unexpected as the days of Noah and Lot
teachings.quote: For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be. For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together. ¶ Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory. And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other. Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors. Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled. Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away. ¶ But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only. But as the days of Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be. For as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark, And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

teachings.text: One will be taken and one left
teachings.quote: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left. Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

teachings.text: From the throne declares he makes all things new — promises the water of life to the thirsty and warns the unfaithful of the lake of fire
Warns the disciples not to chase rumors of his return — they will long for one of his days but not see it
teachings.quote: The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it. And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after them, nor follow them.

teachings.text: Promises the Twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the tribes of Israel when the Son of Man reigns
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

teachings.text: Watch — you do not know the day or hour; be on guard that hearts are not weighed down with the cares of life
teachings.quote: ¶ Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.

teachings.text: The faithful and unfaithful servant — who gives food at the proper time versus who abuses his master's absence
teachings.quote: Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season? Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing. Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods. But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken; The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of, And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

teachings.text: The ten virgins — five wise with oil prepared, five foolish who ran out; the door was shut — be ready
teachings.quote: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom. And five of them were wise, and five were foolish. They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them: But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps. While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept. And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him. Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps. And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out. But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves. And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut. Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us. But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not. Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.

teachings.text: I am coming soon — blessed is the one who keeps these words; behold, I come quickly
teachings.quote: Behold, I come quickly: blessed is he that keepeth the sayings of the prophecy of this book. And, behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be. Surely I come quickly.

teachings.text: Interrupts the bowl-judgment vision — he comes like a thief; blessed is the one who stays alert and keeps his garments
Calls disciples to be dressed and ready with lamps lit — blessed are the servants their master finds awake
teachings.quote: Let your loins be girded about, and your lights burning; And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord, when he will return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open unto him immediately. Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them. And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants. And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through. Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.

subcategories.title: The Fig Tree and the Unknown Hour

teachings.text: From the fig tree learn its lesson — when the branch is tender, summer is near; when these things happen, the end is near
teachings.quote: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.

teachings.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but his words will not; no one knows that day or hour — not the angels, not even the Son
teachings.quote: Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away. ¶ But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only. But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up. Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.

subcategories.title: The Certainty and Scope of Judgment

teachings.text: The Son of Man will come in glory with his angels and repay each according to his works; the Father has given all judgment to the Son
teachings.quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.

teachings.text: People will give account for every careless word; the word he has spoken will judge those who reject him on the last day
teachings.quote: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.

teachings.text: It will be more tolerable for Sodom than for those who reject his messengers; all in the tombs will come out — the good to life, the evil to judgment
teachings.quote: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
